headline: Malaysia Coast Guard Intercepts 28 Tonnes of Smuggled Goods
context: MMEA intercepted 28 tonnes of smuggled goods valued at RM180 million in the Strait of Malacca during 2025 operations.

cards.big: MMEA seized 28 tonnes of smuggled goods worth RM180 million in 2025
cards.sub: Cigarettes diesel fuel and foodstuffs comprised 85% of seized contraband

cards.big: 12 major smuggling syndicates disrupted through 480 maritime operations
cards.sub: Smuggling networks used 150 modified fishing vessels to transport goods

cards.big: Each vessel capable of carrying 2-4 tonnes of contraband per trip
cards.sub: Malaysian territorial waters have 45 identified smuggling corridors

cards.big: Joint patrols with Indonesian navy covered only 35% of corridors
cards.sub: Revenue loss from maritime smuggling estimated at RM2.8 billion annually

cards.big: Smuggling interdiction tests whether maritime enforcement resources match the scale of illicit trade
28 tonnes of smuggled goods intercepted. Coast guard interdiction is the visible fraction of a larger flow.
